title: hieracium longifolium subsp. neocerinthoides
intro: Hieracium longifolium subsp. neocerinthoides, also known as Hawkweed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be beneficial for wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The health benefits of Hieracium longifolium subsp. neocerinthoides include its ability to boost the immune system, protect against radiation damage, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Hawkweed is typically harvested in the summer months, dried, and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
